category_title: Students who prioritize learning and demonstrating ability through effective strategy use
minimal_context: The student is working on a geometry assignment that involves reading instructions to color and mark the angles and sides of triangles.
dialogue:
TUTOR: For this geometry assignment, please read the paragraph, then color and mark the angles and sides of the triangles.

STUDENT: I want to master this task and get a high grade. I'm going to turn this assignment into a game to help me focus.

TUTOR: Go ahead and start reading the instructions.

STUDENT: I am marking the main ideas in the paragraph to help me with the reading. Looking at my first triangle, I can evaluate my work and see I marked the wrong angle.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do next?

STUDENT: I know I have some difficulties reading these long instructions. I tried marking the main ideas, but I'm still confused and my strategy isn't really working to help me figure out what to do next.

options.A: Provide tasks slightly below grade level with visual components like coloring and marking triangles.
options.B: Allow the student to work at their own pace and take breaks whenever desired.
options.C: Respond by expressing exaggerated praise and amazement at how quickly the student is working.
options.D: Constantly provide brief verbal affirmations of correctness and praise throughout the geometry assignment.
